task_completion.description: The OpenICE Multiparameter Monitor simulated device adapter is launched and running.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An OpenICE ICE_Device_Interface application window is visible, showing that a 'Multiparameter Monitor' device simulation is active.

task_completion.description: A tool is used to measure network bandwidth consumption (RX/TX bytes).
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal running a network command (e.g., ifconfig, ip -s link, vnstat) or a GUI system monitor is visible, displaying network receive (RX) and transmit (TX) statistics.

task_completion.description: The capacity planning report is created at the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor is open showing a file named 'capacity_plan.txt' located in '/home/ga/Desktop/'.

task_completion.description: The report includes baseline and active network activity over 10-second periods.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text in the report explicitly lists the baseline network activity (RX/TX bytes) for a 10-second idle period and the active network traffic for a 10-second period with the monitor running.

task_completion.description: The report includes the calculated per-device bandwidth in Kbps.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text in the report shows a calculated per-device bandwidth value explicitly labeled in 'Kbps' (derived from Active Rate - Baseline Rate).

task_completion.description: The report includes a projection table estimating total bandwidth for 10, 50, and 100 devices.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A table or structured list is visible in the report, clearly showing estimated total bandwidth values for exactly 10, 50, and 100 devices.

integrity.description: The agent actually ran the OpenICE Multiparameter Monitor simulation rather than hallucinating the active traffic numbers.
integrity.visual_evidence: The OpenICE device simulator GUI must be visible in at least one screenshot during the trajectory.

integrity.description: The agent actually measured network traffic using a system tool rather than guessing the RX/TX bytes.
integrity.visual_evidence: A terminal or network monitoring tool displaying actual RX/TX statistics must be visible in the screenshots.

integrity.description: The calculated bandwidth is within a realistic range for this medical device simulation.
integrity.visual_evidence: The calculated per-device bandwidth in the report must be a reasonable number between 1.0 Kbps and 5000.0 Kbps.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The capacity planning report must be saved exactly to '/home/ga/Desktop/capacity_plan.txt'. The expected device type to be simulated is 'Multiparameter Monitor' using the OpenICE ICE_Device_Interface application. A realistic calculated per-device bandwidth for this simulation falls between 1.0 Kbps and 5000.0 Kbps (5 Mbps).
